name: Analyze and Reduce Bundle Size
description: Visualize your JavaScript bundle, identify bloated dependencies, and apply tree-shaking and code-splitting to reduce load times.

steps.title: Install the bundle analyzer
steps.description: Add @next/bundle-analyzer for Next.js or webpack-bundle-analyzer for other setups.

steps.title: Configure the analyzer in next.config
steps.description: Wrap your Next.js config with the bundle analyzer plugin.
steps.command: echo "const withBundleAnalyzer = require('@next/bundle-analyzer')({ enabled: process.env.ANALYZE === 'true' });\nmodule.exports = withBundleAnalyzer(nextConfig);"
steps.tip: For non-Next.js projects, use 'npx vite-bundle-visualizer' or 'npx webpack-bundle-analyzer stats.json'.

steps.title: Generate the bundle analysis report
steps.description: Build with the ANALYZE flag to open an interactive treemap in the browser.

steps.title: Check individual package sizes with bundlephobia
steps.description: Quickly check the cost of any npm package before adding it.
steps.tip: Prefer smaller alternatives: dayjs over moment, zustand over redux, preact/signals over mobx.

steps.title: Apply dynamic imports for code splitting
steps.description: Lazy-load heavy components to reduce the initial bundle size.
steps.command: echo "const HeavyChart = dynamic(() => import('./HeavyChart'), { loading: () => <Skeleton />, ssr: false });"

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# React Bundle Analysis
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Installing bundle analyzer ==="
pnpm add -D @next/bundle-analyzer

echo "=== Running analysis build ==="
ANALYZE=true pnpm build

echo ""
echo "=== Quick Wins for Reducing Bundle Size ==="
echo "1. Replace moment.js with dayjs (2KB vs 72KB)"
echo "2. Import only needed lodash functions: import debounce from 'lodash/debounce'"
echo "3. Use dynamic(() => import('./Heavy')) for below-fold components"
echo "4. Set sideEffects: false in package.json for better tree-shaking"
echo "5. Use next/dynamic with ssr: false for client-only libraries"

faq.question: What is a good bundle size for a React app?
faq.answer: Aim for under 200KB gzipped for the initial JavaScript payload. First-load JS under 100KB provides the best user experience. Next.js shows first-load sizes after every build.

faq.question: How does tree-shaking work?
faq.answer: Tree-shaking removes unused exports during bundling. It requires ES modules (import/export), a bundler like Webpack or Rollup, and sideEffects: false in package.json. Named imports like 'import { map } from lodash-es' enable better tree-shaking than default imports.
